pure_fr.evidence: Output A's FR is not atomic, bundling multiple sub-functions (sensor acquisition, hazard detection, model publication) and includes some solution hints, reducing solution neutrality and atomicity. It provides a testability idea but requires further decomposition. Output B presents a more atomic, solution-neutral FR focused on capturing and representing spatial features, with clear sub-FRs and a direct verification concept, better satisfying pure FR criteria.

fr_dp_bridge.evidence: Both outputs present a well-structured DP that addresses the FR with clear decoupling between real-time local processing and cloud or centralized modeling, respecting the what-vs-how boundary. Output A emphasizes a hybrid edge-cloud DP with explicit latency and safety considerations, while Output B defines a centralized hybrid baseline-plus-delta DP with confidence scoring and clear ownership, both minimizing coupling and complexity. Each identifies and mitigates diffusion risks and coupling through well-defined interfaces and synchronization protocols, though both require further refinement of synchronization and parameterization details.

dp_im_bridge.evidence: Output A shows strong containment of onboard sensor fusion and hazard detection within Vehicle (containment score 0.92) with minor diffusion risks related to cloud synchronization. Output B demonstrates excellent containment of the hybrid baseline-plus-delta spatial modeling DP fully within NGHE Solution (containment score 0.92) and clearly separates Vehicle and other modules as consumers, minimizing diffusion. Output B also provides more thorough risk identification and mitigation strategies to prevent DP diffusion.

pure_im.evidence: Output A provides detailed module containment scores with a high containment score (0.92) and low diffusion (0.08) for the NGHE Solution IM, supported by explicit sub-module decomposition recommendations and well-defined interfaces, demonstrating strong structural modularity and stability. Output B also shows strong containment (0.92) for Vehicle IM with clear interface definitions but has moderate diffusion risks due to unclear cloud-side model ownership and synchronization protocols, indicating slightly less mature modular structure.

fr_im_bridge.evidence: Output A clearly defines a single authoritative IM (NGHE Solution) owning the full spatial modeling DP with well-specified interfaces, ownership boundaries, and verifiability at the IM boundary, achieving high containment and minimal diffusion. Output B splits ownership between Vehicle (onboard fusion) and NGHE Solution (cloud modeling) but leaves cloud-side ownership ambiguous and synchronization protocols underspecified, reducing clarity and verifiability at the FR-IM boundary.
